chapter_title.ar: بَابُ أَنَّ الْإِمَامَ ( عليه السلام ) يَعْرِفُ الْإِمَامَ الَّذِي يَكُونُ مِنْ بَعْدِهِ وَ أَنَّ قَوْلَ اللَّهِ تَعَالَى إِنَّ اللَّهَ يَأْمُرُكُمْ أَنْ تُؤَدُّوا الْأَماناتِ إِلى أَهْلِها فِيهِمْ ( عليهم السلام ) نَزَلَتْ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 59 – The Imams<sup>asws</sup> recognises the Imams<sup>as</sup> who would happen to be from after him<sup>asws</sup>, and that the Words of Allah<sup>azwj</sup> the Exalted [4:58] Surely Allah Commands you to make over trusts to their owners, it was Revealed regarding them<sup>asws</sup>

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (182/ 3)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (182/ 3)
text: مَا مَاتَ عَالِمٌ حَتَّى يُعْلِمَهُ اللَّهُ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ إِلَى مَنْ يُوصِي .

en.hubeali: Ahmad, from Muhammad Bin Abdul Jabbar, from Abu Abdullah Al Barqy, from Fazalat Bin Ayoub, from Suleyman Bin Khalid,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> having said: ‘A scholar (Imam<sup>asws</sup>) does not pass away until Allah<sup>azwj</sup> Mighty and Majestic Lets him<sup>asws</sup> know to whom he<sup>asws</sup> should be bequeathing to’.
en.sarwar: 7. Ahmad has narrated from Muhammad ibn ‘Abd al-Jabbar from abu ‘Abdallah al-Barqi from Fudalah ibn Ayyub from Sulayman ibn Khalid from abu ‘Abdallah (a.s.) who has said the following. “No scholar (Imam) has ever died before Allah, the Most Holy, the Most High, would grant him the knowledge of to who must he deliver his will.”
